sections.paragraph:
m. (from the root יָסַר)-

(1) correction of children by their parents, of nations by kings, of men by God. Pro 22:15, שֵׁבֶט מוּסָר; 23:13 , אַל־תִּמְנַע מִנַּעַר מוּסָר “withhold not correction from a child.” Job 12:18, מוּסַר מְלָכִים פִּתֵּחַ “he looses or dissolves the correction (or discipline) of kings,” i.e. their authority. Job 5:17, מוּסַר שַׁדַּי “the correction of the Almighty.” Hos 5:2, “I (will be) a correction to all.”

(2) admonition, discipline, especially that which children receive from parents, men from God, Psa 50:17 Jer 2:30 Pro 1:8 4:1 5:12 8:33 and hence example, by which others are admonished, Eze 5:15 compare the verb, Eze 23:48.

(3) instruction, doctrine, in which sense it is joined with דַּעַת, חָכְמָה, Pro 1:2 4:13 6:23 23:23 .
